rows.name: Jumping Medium Kick
rows.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Great horizontal range for a jump-in; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Great horizontal range for a jump-in; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Neutral Jumping Heavy Kick
rows.notes: Strong and fast air-to-air; lower body projectile invuln 6-19f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Strong and fast air-to-air; lower body projectile invuln 6-19f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

rows.name: Kazekama Shin Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; using 236MK~6LK will counter-hit any opponent button; causes a +32 Hard Knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (gives good oki but prevents follow-up links)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; using 236MK~6LK will counter-hit any opponent button; causes a +32 Hard Knockdown on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (gives good oki but prevents follow-up links)

rows.name: Gorai Axe Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 4-21, allowing it to dodge some low attacks; due to its slow startup, opponent can easily switch from low block to high block to cover both mixup options; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG bounce state (can only follow up with SA3 cancel if the juggle was started with the 6MK follow-up)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 4-21, allowing it to dodge some low attacks; due to its slow startup, opponent can easily switch from low block to high block to cover both mixup options; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle OTG bounce state (can only follow up with SA3 cancel if the juggle was started with the 6MK follow-up)

rows.name: Senka Snap Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; better KD Adv. after 236HK launch; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox (236LK -23/-35; 236MK -22/-31; 236HK KD/-26)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; better KD Adv. after 236HK launch; whiffs on crouching opponents unless they extend a vertical hurtbox (236LK {{sf6-adv|VM|-23/-35}}; 236MK {{sf6-adv|VM|-22/-31}}; 236HK KD/{{sf6-adv|VM|-26}})

rows.name: Kazekama Shin Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; +7 Punish Counter only possible if first hit whiffs; always a true blockstring even if delayed

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; +7 Punish Counter only possible if first hit whiffs; always a true blockstring even if delayed

rows.name: Gorai Axe Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 4f gap between hits on block, good for setting up trade combos (up to 10f gap if delayed); causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 5-18, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 4f gap between hits on block, good for setting up trade combos (up to 10f gap if delayed); causes OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 5-18, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

rows.name: Senka Snap Kick
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; whiffs on crouching opponents (-18 vs. crouch block); true blockstring vs. stand block, can be delayed to create up to 3f gap or to connect on high-juggled opponents

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; whiffs on crouching opponents (-18 vs. crouch block); true blockstring vs. stand block, can be delayed to create up to 3f gap or to connect on high-juggled opponents

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (LK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; 2f gap between final 2 hits on block; (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (MK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; final 2 hits are a true blockstring; (refers to scaled damage)

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; final 2 hits are a true blockstring; (refers to scaled damage)

rows.name: Kasai Thrust Kick (HK)
rows.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; only combos on standing opponents because the 6HK whiffs on crouchers; 7f gap between final 2 hits on stand block (20f if 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouching); (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

raw.notes: Follow-up can be input on hit/block/whiff; only combos on standing opponents because the 6HK whiffs on crouchers; 7f gap between final 2 hits on stand block (20f if 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouching); (refers to scaled damage); starter scaling reduces the effectiveness of fishing for frame trap into SA2 cancel

rows.name: Quick Dash
rows.notes: Can input follow-ups from 11-31f; follow-ups will not reverse direction if crossing under opponent; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration; when inputting with 5KKK takes priority over OD special moves (does not apply when inputting with 5KK after performing a special move input); Run Distance: 1.37 (early stop) / 2.92 (max)

raw.notes: Can input follow-ups from 11-31f; follow-ups will not reverse direction if crossing under opponent; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration; when inputting with 5KKK takes priority over OD special moves (does not apply when inputting with 5KK after performing a special move input); Run Distance: 1.37 (early stop) / 2.92 (max)

rows.name: Emergency Stop
rows.notes: 27f is fastest total Run~Stop time; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration

raw.notes: 27f is fastest total Run~Stop time; Ken is in counter-hit state for entire duration

rows.name: Thunder Kick
rows.notes: Ken's only overhead outside of Jinrai Kick follow-ups; notably can't benefit from Drive Rush like other overheads, but can be used in meaty setups (+4) to link afterwards; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 10-20, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

raw.notes: Ken's only overhead outside of Jinrai Kick follow-ups; notably can't benefit from Drive Rush like other overheads, but can be used in meaty setups (+4) to link afterwards; lifts Ken's leg hurtbox on frames 10-20, allowing it to dodge some low attacks

rows.name: Forward Step Kick
rows.notes: Acts like a Normal Attack for Super canceling (including no extra damage scaling when canceled to SA3); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can be spaced safely in neutral, or used as a frame trap out of 5HP~Run (allowing a link on counter-hit); extends a forward hurtbox during recovery

raw.notes: Acts like a Normal Attack for Super canceling (including no extra damage scaling when canceled to SA3); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; can be spaced safely in neutral, or used as a frame trap out of 5HP~Run (allowing a link on counter-hit); extends a forward hurtbox during recovery

rows.name: Run~Shoryuken
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; final hit whiffs vs. crouch block (1f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

raw.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; final hit whiffs vs. crouch block (1f worse advantage, 75 less chip dmg); cannot hit cross-up

rows.name: Run~Tatsumaki Senpukyaku
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; alternates between forward/backward hits; backwards hitbox can pull opponent towards Ken from behind; CH/PC advantage carries through all hits, making it a great punish starter against very unsafe moves; leaves Ken at close range for a strike/throw mixup on grounded hits; () refers to knockdown advantage when canceled from 5MP~HP

raw.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; alternates between forward/backward hits; backwards hitbox can pull opponent towards Ken from behind; CH/PC advantage carries through all hits, making it a great punish starter against very unsafe moves; leaves Ken at close range for a strike/throw mixup on grounded hits; () refers to knockdown advantage when canceled from 5MP~HP

rows.name: Run~Dragonlash Kick
rows.damage_scaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling
rows.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; properties similar to 623KK (startup, hits twice in juggles, side switch, 2f better advantage vs. crouch); lower OTG bounce height than 623KK; functional startup vs. standing opponents is 11+20f

raw.dmgScaling: Combo (5% extra); both hits apply scaling
raw.notes: Meterless only, does not vary by button strength; properties similar to 623KK (startup, hits twice in juggles, side switch, 2f better advantage vs. crouch); lower OTG bounce height than 623KK; functional startup vs. standing opponents is 11+20f

rows.name: Neutral Taunt
rows.notes: "Bring it!"

raw.notes: "Bring it!"

rows.name: Forward Taunt
rows.notes: "What's up, having second thoughts?"

raw.notes: "What's up, having second thoughts?"

rows.name: Back Taunt
rows.notes: "Now's not the time for jokes."

raw.notes: "Now's not the time for jokes."
